recalls.Description:
Yamaha has issued a recall for its 2021-2025 Drive 2 Personal Transportation Vehicles (PTVs), which include both gas and electric models. These golf cars, available in various colors, may pose a significant risk of injury or death due to the absence of stop lights. The model name and serial number can be found on the vehicle's sides, with serial numbers located in different areas depending on the model. If you need help finding your serial number, reach out to Yamaha for assistance. 
The recall affects specific serial number ranges for each model year. Consumers are urged to check their vehicles against these ranges to determine if they are impacted by this recall.
recalls.Title: Yamaha Recalls 2021-2025 Golf Car Vehicles Due to Serious Crash Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Contact Yamaha at 866-747-4027 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET daily, via email at ygc_callcenter@yamaha-motor.com, or visit https://www.yamahamotorsports.com/Content.php?content=cpsc-recalls for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Yamaha Drive 2 J2D Super Hauler

Retailers.Name: Available at Yamaha dealers across the country from June 2020 to July 2025, priced between $8,300 and $12,900.

Importers.Name: Yamaha Golf Car Company, of Kennesaw, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to lack of stop lights, leading to crash hazards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled golf car vehicles immediately and contact an authorized Yamaha dealer for free stoplight installation. Yamaha will arrange transportation for those unable to visit a dealer.

recalls.metaDescription: Yamaha recalls 2021-2025 golf car vehicles due to serious crash hazards from missing stop lights. Immediate action required.

recalls.Description: Yamaha has issued a recall for its 2017-2024 DR2A and DR2E Personal Transportation Vehicles (PTVs) that have serial numbers starting with J0D, J0E, and J2D. These golf car vehicles, available in various colors, pose a significant risk of injury or death due to potential braking failures. The serial number can be found on the handlebars, located on the left side of the rear seat row.
recalls.Title: Yamaha Recalls 2017-2024 Golf Car Vehicles Due to Serious Crash Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Yamaha at 866-747-4027, available from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, seven days a week. You can also reach them via email at ygc_callcenter@yamaha-motor.com or visit https://www.yamahamotorsports.com/Content.php?content=cpsc-recalls or https://yamaha-motor.com/ and click on 'CPSC Recalls' for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled DR2A and DR2E Golf Cars

Retailers.Name: Available at Yamaha dealers nationwide from June 2018 to July 2025, priced between $9,500 and $13,000.

Importers.Name: Yamaha Golf Car Company, of Kennesaw, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to braking failure

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled vehicles and contact an authorized Yamaha dealer for free brake shoe and pad installation. Yamaha will also provide transportation or install the parts at the vehicle's location if necessary.

recalls.metaDescription: Yamaha recalls 2017-2024 golf car vehicles due to serious crash hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description:
Torquay eTrading LLC is recalling Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Essential Values Grip & Rip re-gripping kits due to potential poisoning hazards. These products, which come in an 8-ounce white bottle with a blue label, were marketed as 'Safe & Non-Toxic' but do not include necessary cautionary warnings about petroleum distillates. This mislabeling violates the Federal Hazardous Substances Act and poses significant risks to consumers, especially children.
Consumers who have purchased these products should stop using them immediately. The kits were available in various configurations, including 2-pack, 4-pack, and 6-pack options, along with a bundle containing 28 golf strips.
recalls.URL: https://www.cpsc.gov/Recalls/2024/Torquay eTrading Recalls Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and-Golf-Grip-Kits-Due-to-Risk-of-Poisoning-Violation-of-the-Federal-Hazardous-Substances-Act
recalls.Title: Recall of Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Kits Due to Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Torquay eTrading LLC at 347-587-8964 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also email recall@essentialvalues.us or visit https://www.essentialvalues.us/pages/recall for more details.

Products.Name: Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip & Rip Kits
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 26,000 (In addition, about 3,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Grip and Rip Re-Gripping Solution Kit with 14 Strips of Grip Tape
Back of the Recalled Golf Grip Solution

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, eBay.com, Walmart.com, and www.essentialvalues.com from December 2017 through May 2024, priced around $9, with kits including golf grip tape costing about $13.

Distributors.Name: Torquay eTrading LLC (dba Essential Values), of New York (formerly sold by Mocer LLC and Bright Ventures LLC)

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to misleading labeling as 'Safe and Non-Toxic' without proper cautionary statements.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the products and contact Torquay eTrading for a free corrected label.

recalls.metaDescription: Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Kits recalled due to poisoning risk. Contact Torquay eTrading for a free corrected label.
